subject: Cloud Computing
title: Linux: The Cloud's Operating System
description: A 60-second lesson on Linux: The Cloud's Operating System.

hook.text: A Finnish student posted his hobby project online in 1991. It now runs 96% of cloud servers.

buildup.text: Linus Torvalds created Linux as a free alternative to expensive Unix systems.

discovery.text: Linux is free, customizable, and endlessly adaptable — everything cloud providers need.

twist.text: Microsoft once called Linux a cancer. Now Azure runs more Linux than Windows.

climax.text: Without Linux, cloud computing as we know it wouldn't exist. It's the invisible backbone.

punchline.text: A student's side project powers the modern internet.

quiz.question: What percentage of cloud servers run Linux?
